## Boltzmann Rationality Applied to Reward Design {.nav-rewards visibility="uncounted"}

Hand crafted rewards are likely to the extent that they lead to high **true** utility in the environment.

::: {.callout-tip title="Definition: Approximately Optimal Designer"}
Allow $w$ to scalarize features $\phi(\tau)$ for reward $R = w^T\phi(\tau)$, then
\begin{align}
P(\tilde{w} \mid w^*, M) \propto \exp\left(\alpha~\mathbb{E}[{w^*}^T\phi(\tau) \mid \tau \sim \pi(\cdot \mid \tilde{w}, M)]\right)
\end{align}
:::

We can then use Bayes rule to develop a posterior over reward functions, given hand-crafted $\tilde{w}$ and confidence $\alpha$.

Lastly, risk-averse planning can be used to select trajectories that would maximize return of the true reward in the worst case.

[@ird]{style="font-size: 0.6em;"}.
